window: CY2023 same-year: EC dateOfSubmission 2023 vs DPIIT IEM Part A Jan-Dec 2023
iem_source: DPIIT IEM Statistics Report Sep-2024, Annexure-3 (industry-wise Part A/B)
ec_source: PARIVESH 2.0 advanceSearchData majorClearanceType=1, filtered to 2023 submissions
universe_caveat: The two registers barely overlap. Of the 14,316 EC proposals filed in 2023, 8,827 (62%) are MINING, which files no IEM at all; most IEM industries (electricals, machinery, textiles, food processing) sit below EIA thresholds and need no EC. This table is only the heavy/polluting manufacturing sliver where both instruments apply.

iem_industries_mapped: Paper and pulp including paper products

reading: Even in the heavy-manufacturing overlap, EC filings outnumber IEM filings ~5:1 (1,902 vs 395 in 2023). EC counts each project/expansion/lease application and is MANDATORY; IEM is a single voluntary memorandum per new unit and is chronically under-filed. So EC is the more complete census of industrial activity in these sectors -- consistent with the repo's standing finding that IEM under-measures investment. Metallurgy 7.6x and chemicals 4.8x are the widest gaps; pulp & paper 0.2x is the lone inversion (few new EC-triggering paper projects, but IEMs still filed).
